overview_summary: Among the four profiled neighbors, one has openly opposed a local development (an animal breeding facility), while the others have no documented stance on development or energy projects. None are elected officials or major public figures; most carry low community influence, with only one neighbor engaged in local issues through her professional role.

neighbors.name: Kenneth A. Bowen
neighbors.claims: Kenneth A. Bowen is listed as an owner of parcel 106-06-029D in Cochise County, but his name does not appear in local news or civic records related to development issues ([cochise.az.gov](https://cochise.az.gov/FAQ.aspx?QID=184#:~:text=Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20,lot%20of%20information%2C%20including%20historical)). No public statements or community leadership roles involving Bowen were found in available sources ([www.casemine.com](https://www.casemine.com/judgement/us/5914b2ebadd7b04934762d52/amp#:~:text=RESOLUTION%20TRUST%20CORPORATION%20v,affidavit%20of%20return%20of%20service)).

approach_recommendations.engage: Approach through a friendly, one-on-one conversation to learn his views. Use a local acquaintance to introduce the project and invite his questions in a low-key setting.

neighbors.name: John & Gail Christmann
neighbors.claims: John and Gail Christmann jointly own parcel 106-06-018F in Cochise County ([cochise.az.gov](https://cochise.az.gov/FAQ.aspx?QID=184#:~:text=Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20,lot%20of%20information%2C%20including%20historical)). They have no recorded presence in public meetings or media reports, and no evidence of them taking any stance on development or land-use matters was found ([www.casemine.com](https://www.casemine.com/judgement/us/5914b2ebadd7b04934762d52/amp#:~:text=RESOLUTION%20TRUST%20CORPORATION%20v,affidavit%20of%20return%20of%20service)).

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage the Christmanns informally via a neighborly visit. Listen to any concerns they might have and share how the development plans respect local land and community values.

neighbors.name: Brenden & Amber Linendoll
neighbors.claims: Amber Linendoll works as a Cochise County animal control officer and in 2021 she formally opposed a proposed puppy and kitten breeding facility in Elfrida, voicing concerns about strain on local shelters ([thisistucson.com](https://thisistucson.com/news/local/article_6cdfdc18-b1ac-11eb-8792-37fe774e2a26.html?mode=nowapp#:~:text=Animal%20control%20officer%20Amber%20Linendoll%2C,to%20pick%20up%20the%20dogs)). Brenden and Amber Linendoll co-own parcel 106-06-029B; apart from Amber’s professional role and her stance in that case, neither is publicly involved in other development disputes ([thisistucson.com](https://thisistucson.com/news/local/article_6cdfdc18-b1ac-11eb-8792-37fe774e2a26.html?mode=nowapp#:~:text=Animal%20control%20officer%20Amber%20Linendoll%2C,to%20pick%20up%20the%20dogs)).

approach_recommendations.engage: Have a community liaison (such as a local law enforcement colleague) reach out to Amber to discuss the project, emphasizing transparency and how it addresses any animal or environmental safety concerns.

neighbors.name: Zane Daniel Ritenour
neighbors.claims: Zane Daniel Ritenour is listed as the owner of parcel 106-06-018R ([cochise.az.gov](https://cochise.az.gov/FAQ.aspx?QID=184#:~:text=Frequently%20Asked%20Questions%20,lot%20of%20information%2C%20including%20historical)). There are no public records of Ritenour participating in local meetings or expressing opinions on development or energy projects, indicating no known civic profile ([www.casemine.com](https://www.casemine.com/judgement/us/5914b2ebadd7b04934762d52/amp#:~:text=RESOLUTION%20TRUST%20CORPORATION%20v,affidavit%20of%20return%20of%20service)).

approach_recommendations.engage: Reach out in a casual, neighborly manner to build rapport and understand any concerns he may have. Emphasize that the project will be mindful of local property interests and invite his input early on.
